_notes: Each incident maps to a card in the Incidents page and feeds the MIM Command Room and MIM Report. id must be unique. pri: P1|P2|P3. status: Open|In Progress|Resolved|Auto-Resolved. Each incident now includes an 'intel' block containing rootCause, causalChain, recommendation, similar incidents, agents, autoFixable, confidence, and risk. This replaces INCIDENT_INTEL from incidents_ericsson.js.

incidents.notes: OOM kill confirmed — -Xmx 2g insufficient for monorepo builds. Nexus disk pressure identified as upstream cause. L3 DevOps paged.
incidents.desc: GitLab CI runners on Stockholm DC K8s cluster experiencing OOM kills — JVM heap exhausted during Gradle dependency resolution on large monorepo builds. 2,400 R&D engineers blocked. 847 pipeline jobs queued. Build queue growing 40 jobs/minute. Nexus Artifactory disk at 91% causing dependency resolution timeouts that trigger runner heap exhaustion.

intel.rootCause: GitLab CI runner JVM heap exhausted by large monorepo Gradle builds — -Xmx 2g insufficient. Upstream cause: Nexus Artifactory disk at 91% causing dependency resolution timeouts that force runners to retry, multiplying heap consumption. Root fix requires both Nexus cleanup and runner JVM resize.
causalChain: Nexus Artifactory /data at 91% — SNAPSHOT artifact retention policy disabled 6 weeks ago
Gradle dependency resolution timing out — Nexus I/O pressure causing 30s+ fetch delays
GitLab runners retrying failed fetches — heap growing with each retry cycle
JVM -Xmx 2g exhausted — OOM kill triggered across all large-monorepo builds
2,400 engineers blocked — 847 pipeline jobs queued and growing
intel.recommendation: IMMEDIATE: OneEngine NEXUS-CLEANUP runbook — archive SNAPSHOTs >30 days, recover 400GB+. PARALLEL: Increase runner -Xmx to 4g via K8s ConfigMap rolling restart. MEDIUM-TERM: Enable Nexus retention policy (keep last 5 SNAPSHOTs per component). Add K8s runner HPA — scale 8→24 pods at build queue depth >100.

incidents.notes: Retention policy disabled during last maintenance — not re-enabled. OneEngine artifact cleanup runbook queued pending NOC approval.
incidents.desc: Nexus Artifactory /nexus-data partition at 91% on Stockholm DC. SNAPSHOT artifact retention policy not enforced — 847GB of stale artifacts accumulated over 6 weeks. Dependency resolution for Gradle/Maven builds timing out as Nexus struggles to serve requests under I/O pressure. Direct upstream cause of GitLab runner OOM kills.

intel.rootCause: Nexus Artifactory SNAPSHOT artifact retention policy disabled during maintenance window 6 weeks ago — never re-enabled. 847GB accumulated. Disk I/O saturation causing all dependency resolution to time out — primary upstream cause of GitLab runner OOM kills.
causalChain: Retention policy disabled at maintenance window — post-maintenance checklist incomplete
SNAPSHOT artifacts accumulating — 847GB in 6 weeks at current build cadence
/data partition at 91% — I/O pressure on all artifact reads and writes
Dependency resolution timeouts — cascading to GitLab runner heap exhaustion
intel.recommendation: OneEngine NEXUS-CLEANUP: archive artifacts >30 days to cold storage, delete SNAPSHOTs beyond last 5 per component. Expected recovery: 400GB+. Post-cleanup: re-enable retention policy via Nexus API. Add 70% disk alert threshold.

incidents.notes: SM50 confirms WP saturation. Three batch jobs competing in dialog class. Azure VM CPU 87%. BASIS admin contacted — deferral approval required.
incidents.desc: SAP S/4HANA Finance period-end close blocked — AP1 work processes at 89% saturation on Azure VM Standard_E32s_v5. FI_POST_CLOSE batch running simultaneously with GL_RECON and month-end accrual jobs in dialog WP class. 340 finance users across 47 countries experiencing session timeouts. CFO office escalated directly.

intel.rootCause: SAP S/4HANA AP1 Azure VM work processes saturated by three concurrent month-end batch jobs (FI_POST_CLOSE, GL_RECON, accrual processing) all scheduled in dialog WP class. Azure Standard_E32s_v5 CPU at 87%. Period-end close window has insufficient WP capacity for concurrent batch + dialog load.
causalChain: Month-end: FI_POST_CLOSE + GL_RECON + accrual batch all scheduled simultaneously in dialog WP class
89/100 dialog WPs occupied — finance users queuing for WP slots
Azure VM CPU 87% — no headroom for additional WP requests
340 finance users across 47 countries experiencing session timeouts
CFO office escalated — period-end close deadline at risk
intel.recommendation: IMMEDIATE: Defer GL_RECON to background WP class via SM37 — 15-minute impact. Defer accrual batch to 20:00. Monitor WP after deferral. MEDIUM-TERM: Azure VM resize to E48s_v5 for period-end windows. PERMANENT: Batch scheduling policy — month-end jobs must use background WP class.

incidents.notes: Policy deployment at 00:45 correlates to latency onset. Recursive evaluation confirmed in Entra audit logs. Rollback prepared.
incidents.desc: Azure Entra ID MFA latency elevated — token issuance averaging 4.2s vs 280ms baseline. Conditional access policy deployed by security team at 00:45 triggering recursive policy evaluation loop on legacy B2B guest accounts. 8,000 employees in Stockholm and Kista affected at morning login window. Teams, GitLab, Jira all depend on Entra for SSO.

intel.rootCause: Entra ID conditional access policy deployed at 00:45 contains recursive evaluation loop on legacy B2B guest accounts — each MFA request triggers 47 policy evaluations instead of 3. Token issuance latency 4.2s vs 280ms baseline. 8,000 Stockholm/Kista employees affected at morning login window.
causalChain: Security team deployed new conditional access policy at 00:45 — pre-production testing on non-B2B accounts only
Policy contains recursive evaluation condition on legacy B2B guest accounts
Each MFA request triggers 47 policy evaluations — 15x normal compute
Token issuance latency 4.2s — SSO for Teams, GitLab, Jira all degraded
8,000 employees affected at 07:00 Stockholm morning login peak
intel.recommendation: IMMEDIATE: Roll back conditional access policy to 00:44 version via Entra admin centre. Monitor token latency — should return to baseline within 5 minutes of rollback. Post-rollback: fix recursive condition in sandbox, re-test against B2B guest accounts before re-deploy.

incidents.notes: Campaign launched on schedule — low manager engagement. Auto-reminder sent. Compliance team preparing manual review list for critical entitlements.
incidents.desc: SailPoint IdentityNow access certification campaign 14 days overdue — 3,847 access entitlements pending review across SAP, GitLab, and Azure AD groups. SOX compliance deadline in 3 days. Export control flagged entitlements not reviewed. Chief Compliance Officer notified.

intel.rootCause: SailPoint IGA access certification campaign launched on schedule but manager engagement rate only 67%. No escalation mechanism configured beyond initial email reminder. SOX-flagged and export-control entitlements mixed with routine entitlements — managers not prioritising. 14-day overdue with 3-day compliance deadline.
causalChain: Quarterly certification campaign launched — 3,847 entitlements across SAP, GitLab, Azure AD
Only email reminders configured — no escalation, no skip-level notification
67% response rate — 1,270 entitlements unreviewed including SOX and export control scope
14 days overdue — Chief Compliance Officer escalated
intel.recommendation: Auto-escalate all unreviewed entitlements to line manager's manager via Teams — not email. For SOX and export control entitlements: auto-revoke after 72h if not reviewed. ZeroOps to generate prioritised review list for compliance team.

incidents.notes: WAL accumulation from inactive analytics slot root cause. DROP REPLICATION SLOT queued. Will clear immediately.
incidents.desc: Jira Software issue load times degraded to 8.4s — SLA threshold 2s. PostgreSQL database on Azure running 94% buffer cache hit rate but 47 long-running queries averaging 12s. Inactive replication slot jira_analytics_slot accumulated 62GB WAL. 1,200 engineers and project managers affected.

intel.rootCause: Jira PostgreSQL database inactive replication slot jira_analytics_slot accumulated 62GB WAL files — analytics consumer disconnected 9 hours ago without dropping its slot. WAL accumulation causing disk I/O pressure and query planner suboptimal plan selection.
causalChain: Analytics consumer disconnected at 14:30 — slot not dropped on disconnect
WAL files accumulating — 62GB in 9 hours
Disk I/O pressure increasing — query planner falling back to sequential scans
47 long-running queries averaging 12s — Jira issue load 8.4s vs 2s SLA
intel.recommendation: DROP REPLICATION SLOT jira_analytics_slot — immediate. Run VACUUM ANALYZE on issues and issuetype tables. WAL will clear within 5 minutes. Add monitoring: slot inactive >30min triggers P3 incident.

incidents.notes: Service account credential expired at 22:00 during automated rotation cycle. Concur sync job authenticating with expired token.
incidents.desc: SAP Concur integration to SAP S/4HANA FI module failing — 2,847 expense reports stuck in sync queue. Service account credential expired at 22:00 during automated rotation cycle. Global traveller population unable to submit expenses for reimbursement.

intel.rootCause: SAP Concur integration service account password expired during automated rotation at 22:00. Rotation script updated Azure Key Vault secret but Concur sync job still using cached credential from previous run. Sync job failing silently — no alert configured on authentication failure.
causalChain: Service account password rotated at 22:00 by automated rotation policy
Concur sync job not notified of rotation — still using cached credential
Authentication failure — sync job failing silently, no alert raised
2,847 expense reports accumulating in queue
intel.recommendation: Renew Concur sync job credential from Key Vault. Restart sync job. Validate 2,847 expense reports delivered. Add: Concur sync job auth failure alert at P2. Add: credential rotation webhook to notify dependent jobs.

incidents.notes: ZeroOps Alert Correlator identified Windows Update KB5036980 as root cause. 46,983 duplicates suppressed. 17 actionable incidents surfaced. Zero engineer involvement.
incidents.desc: CrowdStrike Falcon generated 47,000 alerts in 18 minutes across 100,000 endpoints after Windows Update KB5036980 policy change. ZeroOps Alert Correlator identified the change event, correlated all alerts to single root cause, suppressed 46,983 duplicates, surfaced 17 actionable security incidents. MTTR 6 minutes.

intel.rootCause: Windows Update KB5036980 deployed via Intune policy at 20:00 triggered CrowdStrike Falcon behavioral detection on 100,000 endpoints simultaneously. Alert Correlator identified Intune audit event and correlated all 47,000 alerts to single change event in 6 minutes.
causalChain: Intune policy pushed Windows Update KB5036980 to 100,000 endpoints at 20:00
CrowdStrike Falcon triggered behavioral detections on update activity — 47,000 alerts in 18 minutes
ZeroOps Alert Correlator detected Intune audit event — correlated to alert spike
47,000 alerts → 17 actionable incidents — 46,983 suppressed as change-event noise
intel.recommendation: Resolved automatically. Add pre-notification: Windows Update deployments should pre-register with ZeroOps — suppression rule auto-created 30 minutes before rollout. Security team reviews 17 genuinely anomalous alerts.

incidents.notes: Zscaler Digital Experience confirmed routing issue — APAC traffic hitting EU nodes. Corrected via Zscaler admin portal.
incidents.desc: Zscaler ZPA latency elevated for APAC region — application access averaging 340ms vs 80ms baseline. Engineers in Singapore, Bangalore, Beijing unable to access GitLab and Jira at acceptable speed. Routing issue — APAC traffic hitting EU nodes instead of Singapore PoP.

intel.rootCause: Zscaler ZPA routing misconfiguration after maintenance window — APAC regional policy reverted to pre-maintenance state, routing APAC traffic to EU PoP (Frankfurt) instead of Singapore. 340ms latency vs 80ms baseline.
causalChain: Zscaler maintenance window at 18:00 UTC — routing policy changes applied
Post-maintenance validation missed APAC routing table
APAC traffic routing to Frankfurt PoP — 340ms latency vs 80ms Singapore PoP
Engineers in Singapore, Bangalore, Beijing unable to work at acceptable speed
intel.recommendation: Resolved: Singapore PoP re-enabled in Zscaler admin console. Add post-maintenance validation checklist: ZDX latency check per region mandatory before maintenance sign-off.

incidents.notes: ZeroOps Capacity Planner detected expiry risk during weekly scan. Renewal process initiated. 847 R&D engineers using MATLAB/Simulink/Xilinx tools.
incidents.desc: FlexLM licence server for MATLAB, Simulink and Xilinx tools nearing expiry — 14 days remaining. ZeroOps Capacity Planner detected expiry risk during weekly scan. Auto-notification sent to licence admin team. Renewal process initiated.

intel.rootCause: FlexLM licence server certificate expiring in 14 days — originally set to 3-year term, renewal process requires 10 business days. ZeroOps Capacity Planner detected during weekly expiry scan. Proactive detection with sufficient lead time for manual renewal.
causalChain: FlexLM licence certificate 3-year term set in 2021 — expiry not calendared
No monitoring on licence expiry — would have gone undetected until engineers hit expired licence
ZeroOps weekly scan detected 14 days before expiry
847 engineers using MATLAB/Simulink/Xilinx — expiry would block all HW simulation
intel.recommendation: Resolved proactively. Renewal initiated — 10 business days sufficient. Add to ZeroOps: all FlexLM licence expiries monitored with 30-day and 14-day alerts. Annual calendar reminder for licence renewal owner.
